title: Monitoring a Live Service Log
scenario: A deployment pipeline is running on this server. A background service writes timestamped events to /var/log/service/events.log every few seconds. The platform team needs confirmation that the current deployment completed successfully — specifically, they need the DEPLOY_SUCCESS event captured the moment it appears. The log file is being written continuously; events appear every few seconds and DEPLOY_SUCCESS will appear within approximately 30 seconds.
symptoms: Active deployment pipeline with no dashboard confirmation
Background service writing live events to /var/log/service/events.log
Team needs DEPLOY_SUCCESS event captured as evidence of successful deployment
mission: Preview the log file structure, monitor it live to capture the DEPLOY_SUCCESS event, then take a snapshot of the last 20 lines after the event appears.

hints.title: Previewing a file
hints.content: 'head -5 filename' prints the first 5 lines. The -5 is shorthand for -n 5. Both forms work identically.

hints.title: Following a live file
hints.content: 'tail -f filename' keeps the terminal open and prints new lines as they are appended to the file. Press Ctrl+C to stop when you have seen what you need.

hints.title: Capturing a specific event automatically
hints.content: Combine 'tail -f' with 'grep -m 1' to wait for a pattern and stop as soon as it is found. The -m 1 flag means stop after the first match, so the command exits on its own.

hints.title: Getting the last N lines
hints.content: 'tail -20 filename' prints the last 20 lines. Unlike tail -f, this is a one-shot read with no live following — it reads the current state of the file and exits immediately.

hints.title: Bonus
hints.content: Try 'tail -f /var/log/service/events.log | grep --line-buffered DEPLOY' to filter the live stream so that only lines matching a pattern are shown. The --line-buffered flag ensures output is printed immediately rather than being held in a buffer.

acceptance_criteria.description: /home/intern/log_preview.txt exists and contains the first 5 lines of /var/log/service/events.log
/home/intern/deploy_event.txt exists and contains the DEPLOY_SUCCESS line
/home/intern/log_tail.txt exists and contains at least 5 lines from the end of /var/log/service/events.log
